riddle: I have cities but no houses, forests but no trees, and rivers but no water. What am I?
The more you take of me, the more you leave behind. What am I?
I speak without a mouth and hear without ears. I have no body, but come alive with wind. What am I?
What has a heart that doesn't beat?
I am always in front of you but can't be seen. What am I?
What can travel around the world while staying in a corner?
What has one eye but cannot see?
The person who makes it sells it. The person who buys it never uses it. What is it?
I am not alive, but I grow; I don't have lungs, but I need air; I don't have a mouth, but water kills me. What am I?
Forward I am heavy, backward I am not. What am I?
A man describes his daughter: she has as many brothers as sisters, but each brother has only half as many brothers as sisters. How many sons and daughters does the man have?
What begins with an E, ends with an E, but only ever contains one letter?
The more of me there is, the less you see. What am I?
I have keys but no locks, space but no room, and you can enter but not go inside. What am I?
Two fathers and two sons go fishing. Each catches exactly one fish, yet they bring home only three fish total. How is that possible?
What gets wetter the more it dries?
A man is looking at a portrait. Someone asks whose picture it is. He replies: brothers and sisters I have none, but this man's father is my father's son. Who is in the portrait?
